facts: A black hole's event horizon is about the size of a football field if its mass is 12 million times the Sun's
The gravitational pull would stretch you like spaghetti over a distance of 100,000 football fields
The curvature of space-time would make time pass 1.4 times slower for you compared to Earth during the last 10 seconds of free fall
idea.payoff: and still we don't know what happens next
idea.title: Falling into a Black Hole 🌀

scenes.text: FREE FALLING INTO A BLACK HOLE...
scenes.visual: A stylized animation of a person (silhouette) falling towards a rotating black hole, with a '200s' countdown timer appearing briefly and fading.

scenes.text: EVENT HORIZON: A FOOTBALL FIELD WIDE (for 12M Sun mass)
scenes.visual: A close-up of a black hole's event horizon, with a graphic overlay of a football field for scale. Text describing the mass ratio.
An illustrative animation of a human figure being stretched vertically and thinned like spaghetti as it falls deeper into the black hole. A huge '100,000 Football Fields' graphic expands rapidly.

scenes.text: LAST 10s: TIME PASSES 1.4x SLOWER FOR YOU THAN EARTH
scenes.visual: A split screen: one side showing a clock ticking normally (Earth), the other side showing a clock slowing down and distorting near the black hole (you). Numeric overlay showing the 1.4x difference.

scenes.text: ...AND STILL, WE DON'T KNOW WHAT HAPPENS NEXT.
scenes.visual: The view rapidly zooms into the singularity, becoming a chaotic, abstract swirl of light and color that quickly fades to pure black. A large question mark appears briefly before the screen goes dark.
